cover.title: THE VINE
cover.subtitle: The one who stays calm until everything quietly overflows.
cover.tagline: You look easy going on the outside, but inside, everything keeps accumulating.

profile_scores.caption: High Care · High Openness, Low Drive · Low Expression. You stay unshaken on the surface even when uncomfortable, while it keeps piling up inside.

short_summary: You come across as someone who doesn't get rattled.
That whole time you weren't visibly shaken. It wasn't because you were okay. You were just enduring it the entire time.
What changes love for you is learning to speak while the feeling is still small.

full_report.headline: THE VINE
full_report.intro: You come across as someone who doesn't get rattled. People say being with you is comfortable. You rarely get angry, rarely show displeasure. The person you're with ends up relaxing, not you. And that is where the imbalance begins.

chapters.title: When You Love
body: You may come across as someone who does not get rattled easily.
People often feel comfortable with you. You rarely show anger or displeasure, so the person beside you may relax before you do.
What looks calm is not always comfort.
You may not be as fine as you look.
When something bothers you, hurts, or needs to be said, you may swallow it first.
It can happen so quickly that even you lose track of what you held back.
From the outside, nothing seems wrong.
Inside, each unspoken moment can leave a little more weight behind.
chapters.profile_note: High Care · High Openness, Low Drive · Low Expression. You stay unshaken on the surface even when uncomfortable, while it keeps piling up inside.

chapters.title: The Pattern You Keep Repeating: THE UNSPOKEN OVERFLOW CURVE
body: This pattern may feel familiar.
Early in a relationship, you may seem steady.
You let small differences pass.
You may go along even when you disagree.
You may stay quiet when something hurts.
So the relationship can feel easy for the other person.
The difficulty is that the unspoken feelings do not always disappear.
They can keep gathering.
Then one moment may bring out a decision or reaction that surprises both of you.
To them, it may look sudden.
For you, it may feel like the end of something that had been building for a long time.
The surface can stay calm while the pressure underneath keeps rising.
Eventually, what stayed unspoken may come out all at once.
chapters.highlight: You may have looked steady the whole time, while quietly carrying more than anyone realized.

chart.title: THE UNSPOKEN OVERFLOW CURVE
chart.description: You stay outwardly steady while pressure rises inside, until at some point it all overflows at once.

chapters.title: What You've Gotten Wrong About Yourself

items.truth: You may be easy going in many situations. But sometimes keeping the peace feels safer than showing what you actually want.
items.truth_short: You may seem easy going, while using calmness to protect the relationship.

items.belief: Enduring it is the mature thing to do.
items.truth: Patience can be mature. But when every concern gets pushed back, patience can turn into postponing the conversation.
items.truth_short: Patience helps, but repeated silence can become delay.

items.belief: It's because I'm just less sensitive than others.
items.truth: You may notice discomfort quickly, then smooth over your expression before anyone can see it. That does not mean you are less sensitive.
items.truth_short: You may feel something quickly, then hide it just as quickly.

chapters.title: The Person You Keep Choosing
body: Different people can bring out the same emotional pattern.
Someone with big feelings may pull you into the calm role. Their emotions come out first, while yours keep waiting.
Someone with strong opinions may make decisions feel easier at first. Over time, you may stop checking what you want.
Someone who takes "I'm fine" literally may never ask again. If you do not explain either, the unsaid feeling stays with you.
Someone who processes everything alone may look peaceful beside you. But if neither person speaks, nothing truly gets worked through.
chapters.highlight: A calm relationship and a relationship where both people stay silent are not the same.

chapters.title: What Actually Changes Something
body: What you need is not to become even better at enduring.
What may help is saying small discomforts while they are still small.
Start with the ordinary things.
If the plan does not suit you, say what you would prefer.
It may sound simple, but this can be one of the hardest things for you.
When small preferences stay hidden, larger feelings can become harder to express too.
In a healthier relationship, a small difference does not have to become a crisis.
chapters.action_sentence: Let one small discomfort be heard before it becomes much heavier.

chapters.title: Where You Go From Here
body: You know how to endure for a long time without showing much.
What you may be learning next is how to trust that the relationship can survive a small disagreement.
This is not about saying everything at once.
It is about bringing things up gradually, before they gather too much weight.
That gives both people more time to understand and repair what is happening.
chapters.transition_note: You've spent a long time staying steady on the outside. The next step is learning how to speak before everything gathers inside.

chapters.title: The Types You Keep Falling For

items.type_title: Someone Who Rarely Shows They Are Hurt
items.description: They move on quickly and rarely show pain. At first, that may feel mature. But when both people keep enduring, the real issue can become hard to name.

items.type_title: Someone Who Says Everything Is Fine
items.description: They say they are fine, and so do you. That can feel comfortable. But comfort is not always the same as honesty.

items.type_title: Someone Who Avoids Ruining the Mood
items.description: They would rather wait until tomorrow. Then tomorrow becomes next week. The atmosphere stays calm, while the problem remains.
When something hurts, they think it through alone and return acting normal. If both of you do that, misunderstanding can grow without a real conversation.

chapters.title: The Warning Signs You've Missed
chapters.intro_note: Some of these signs may have appeared early. You may have brushed them aside as too small to mention.

signal_strength.label: Even when you disagree, you often say that everything is fine
Your discomfort is difficult for others to notice, and sometimes difficult for you to name
When something hurts, you think it through alone instead of saying it in the moment
The other person may believe you are rarely upset because so little is shown

chapters.core_signal: The inside keeps gathering weight while the outside continues to look calm.

chapters.title: What a Healthy Relationship Looks Like for You

checklist.title: Saying something small doesn't ruin the mood.
checklist.description: You can name a small concern without the whole mood turning against you.

checklist.title: They notice when you're upset.
checklist.description: They notice changes and ask, but they do not expect you to communicate only through hints.

checklist.title: They don't mistake silence for peace.
checklist.description: Quiet is not treated as proof that everything is fine.

checklist.title: They check in before things pile up.
checklist.description: The two of you speak before hurt has time to gather into something much larger.

chapters.title: What Happens In Your Next Relationship

sections.who_breaks_you: You're vulnerable to someone who accepts every "I'm fine" without looking further. If you keep hiding the discomfort and they keep moving on, the relationship can miss the problem until it feels much bigger.
sections.who_steadies_you: A steadier match is someone who notices changes, asks gently, and can hear a small complaint without becoming defensive. They do not make you carry the whole conversation alone.
sections.same_pattern_repeats: You may still feel drawn to someone who avoids conflict or misses subtle cues. That does not decide the outcome. What matters is whether both of you learn to speak before silence becomes distance.
sections.next_signs: This time, the pattern may show up in a very small pause. You feel the words "I'm fine" arriving before you have checked what you really feel. That pause can become a chance to answer more honestly.
sections.try_this: When something small does not feel right, name it simply in the moment. You might say, "That did not feel good to me." Then keep watching whether both of you can talk, listen, and repair over time.
sections.final_line: What matters is not one reaction. It is whether small truths can be spoken and received safely over time.

action_plan.summary: What you need isn't learning to endure better. You're already far too good at that.
action_items: This week, say one small discomfort before it grows.
When you are about to say everything is fine, pause and name what you actually feel.
Notice whether the relationship makes room for disagreement without turning it into a crisis.

closing.message: You've spent a long time learning not to be shaken. The next report covers why you've always piled things up only on the inside, and how you learn to bring them out sooner.
closing.cross_sell_cta: Curious what your partner's pattern looks like? See their Cosmic Blueprint.
